title: Alert: Water supply in parts of Hyd to be disrupted on July 6,7 - The Hans India
full_text:
Alert: Water supply in parts of Hyd to be disrupted on July 6,7
Hyderabad: Residents in several localities under the Golconda Zone of the Hyderabad Metropolitan Water Supply and Sewerage Board (HMWSSB) are likely to face a 24-hour disruption in drinking water supply on July 6 and 7, as the Water Board undertakes critical repair works at the Allabanda Reservoir in the Goshamahal Circle.
According to the Water Board, heavy leakages have developed in the butterfly valves installed at the inlet and outlet of the 10 ML capacity Allabanda Reservoir. To prevent further water loss and ensure uninterrupted supply in the future, the damaged valves will be replaced with new ones. The repair works will commence at 6 a.m. on Monday, July 6 and continue until 6 a.m. on Tuesday, July 7.
During this period, drinking water supply will remain disrupted in several areas. Areas affected on July 6 include Jhirra, Indira Nagar, Hari Darga, Kummarwadi, Tappachabutra, Akashpuri, Shibli Hills, Bilal Masjid surroundings, Godeki Kabar, Jali, Hanuman, Jangur Basti, Abushatakia, 2J Bus Stop, Ziaguda and Karwan. Areas affected on July 7 include Sabzimandi, Ganga Nagar, Osmania, Shivlal Nagar, Berham Galli, Gangamata, Jali Hanuman, Azam Galli, Rahimpura, Puranapul, Chudi Bazaar, Osmania Hospital surroundings, Shankar Bazaar and Jummerat Bazar. The Water Board has appealed to residents in the affected areas to store adequate water in advance and use it judiciously during the shutdown period. Officials stated that the maintenance work is essential to improve the reliability of the distribution system and prevent recurring leakages at the reservoir. Consumers have also been requested to cooperate with the Water Board until the works are completed and normal water supply is restored.
